spoilers.title: Boruto is just like me😭🙏🏼 Mirai (@MiraiTBV) #BorutoSpoilers Question: Boruto has said that age doesn't matter when it comes to romance, but what age gap is actually within his acceptable range? Ans
spoilers.text: Boruto is just like me😭🙏🏼 Mirai (@MiraiTBV) #BorutoSpoilers Question: Boruto has said that age doesn't matter when it comes to romance, but what age gap is actually within his acceptable range? Answer: If they are younger than his little sister Himawari (who is currently 12 years old), he might mind it a little bit. However, when it comes to someone older, the sky's the limit—no matter how old they are, he genuinely wouldn't care at all! — https://nitter.net/MiraiTBV/status/2060567953569947771#m

misc.title: The Golden Age Arc isn’t just Berserk’s best arc—it’s the foundation of the entire story. It makes you care about the Band of the Hawk, understand Griffith, connect with Guts and Casca, and when the E
misc.text: The Golden Age Arc isn’t just Berserk’s best arc—it’s the foundation of the entire story. It makes you care about the Band of the Hawk, understand Griffith, connect with Guts and Casca, and when the Eclipse finally happens, it hits like a truck because you’ve lived through their journey alongside them. Without Golden Age, Berserk would still be great. Because of Golden Age, it’s a masterpiece.

items.title: The Golden Age Arc isn’t just Berserk’s best arc—it’s the foundation of the entire story. It makes you care about the Band of the Hawk, understand Griffith, connect with Guts and Casca, and when the E
items.text: The Golden Age Arc isn’t just Berserk’s best arc—it’s the foundation of the entire story. It makes you care about the Band of the Hawk, understand Griffith, connect with Guts and Casca, and when the Eclipse finally happens, it hits like a truck because you’ve lived through their journey alongside them. Without Golden Age, Berserk would still be great. Because of Golden Age, it’s a masterpiece.
